results.description: Check unauthenticated exposure of current-user endpoint.
results.conclusion: Endpoint returns null when unauthenticated and does not disclose sensitive account data.

results.observed:
{"error":{"json":{"message":"[
  {
    "origin": "string",
    "code": "too_small",
    "minimum": 2,
    "inclusive": true,
    "path": [
      "fullName"
    ],
    "message": "Too small: expected string to have >=2 characters"
  },
  {
    "origin": "string",
    "code": "invalid_format",
    "format": "email",
    "pattern": "/^(?!\\.)(?!.*\\.\\.)([A-Za-z0-9_'+\\-\\.]*)[A-Za-z0-9_+-]@([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\\-]*\\.)+[A-Za-z]{2,}$...
results.conclusion: Server-side schema validation rejects malformed registration input before business logic.

results.description: Submit oversized registration fields to probe boundary handling.
results.conclusion: Length limits are enforced and the endpoint fails safely instead of crashing.

results.description: Inject classic SQLi pattern in email field.
results.conclusion: The email parser rejects a classic SQLi string before any authentication logic proceeds.

results.description: Inject SQLi-like content into password while keeping other fields valid.
results.conclusion: The backend treats the SQLi-like password as ordinary data and returns a normal invalid-credentials response without SQL error leakage.

results.description: Probe password-policy enforcement with a weak common password.

    "code": "invalid_format",
    "format": "regex",
    "pattern": "/\\d/",
    "path": [
      "newPassword"
    ],
    "message": "Password must contain a num...
results.conclusion: Weak/common-password reset attempts are rejected at schema level.

results.description: Submit an oversized CAPTCHA token to test defensive length checks.
results.conclusion: The published endpoint did not reject the oversized CAPTCHA token at validation level and still returned a normal invalid-credentials response, which suggests CAPTCHA enforcement may be absent or bypassed on this live version.

results.description: Check whether a clearly bogus CAPTCHA token blocks reset-password processing.
results.conclusion: The request proceeds to account lookup and returns a normal missing-account result even with a bogus CAPTCHA token, which strongly suggests CAPTCHA is not enforced on the published endpoint.

results.description: Send malformed JSON structure instead of the expected object body.
results.conclusion: The endpoint returns a controlled client error for malformed bodies rather than crashing.

results.description: Check basic transport/security headers on the published site.
results.observed: {"Date": "Sat, 02 May 2026 18:36:16 GMT", "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8", "Transfer-Encoding": "chunked", "Connection": "keep-alive", "Cache-Control": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate", "Expires": "0", "Strict-Transport-Security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains", "Pragma": "no-cache", "Content-Encoding": "gzip", "Server": "cloudflare", "CF-RAY": "9f592168ae26e9ae-SCL"}
results.conclusion: Published site enforces HSTS at the edge, which is a positive transport-security signal.
